currentProblem.name: The Halting Problem (HALT_TM)
currentProblem.description: Given an encoding of a Turing machine M and a string w, does M halt on input w?
currentProblem.inputFormat: Encoding ⟨M, w⟩ where M is a Turing machine and w is a string over M's input alphabet
currentProblem.question: Does M halt on input w?
yesInstances: ⟨M_accept_all, ε⟩ where M_accept_all transitions immediately to qaccept
⟨M_palindrome, 'aba'⟩ where M_palindrome decides palindromes and 'aba' is one
noInstances: ⟨M_loop, ε⟩ where M_loop has no accepting transitions and loops forever
⟨M_count, '10'⟩ where M_count loops on inputs that are not powers of 2

enumeration.description: Enumerate all Turing machines M₁, M₂, M₃, … using a standard encoding
enumeration.indexSet: ℕ (natural numbers used as machine indices)
enumeration.enumeratedObjects: All Turing machines over a fixed alphabet Σ

dependencies: ability to enumerate all Turing machines
assumptions: Turing machine model captures all effective computation (Church-Turing thesis)
A Turing machine can be encoded as a string over a finite alphabet
keyInsight: Assume decider H exists for HALT_TM. Construct D: on input ⟨M⟩, run H on ⟨M, ⟨M⟩⟩; if H says M halts on ⟨M⟩ then loop, else accept. Running D on ⟨D⟩ yields: D halts iff D does not halt — contradiction. Therefore H cannot exist and HALT_TM is undecidable.
